Leave.—A person, on appointment iis a Presidinp OiT,- cer in an Appellate Tribunal shall be tmillcd lo few a. follows : (0 Earned leave at the rale of thirty days for ever) conjplcled calendar year of s c m c e or a pari thereof: Provided Uiot the leave account shall be credited with earned leave, in advance, in two instalment* v' fifteen days each on the first day of January Bin1 July of every calendar year :, riovldcd that he tleavr account shall b e t r u l i t i d with the close of previous half year shall be carried forward to ' h e next half year, subject to the condition I h n the leave so carried forward phi; credit fi>; Hie hali year d o not exceed th« maxi- m u m limit of one hundred and eighty days ; tiij Half pay leave on medical certificate or on private aifuirs at the rate of twenty days in respect of each completed year of service and ihe leave salary for half pay leave shall be equivalent t o half of Lhs leave salary admissible during the earned leave : (iii) Leave on half pay may he commuted to full p;iy leave at the discretion of the President of India, provided it is taken on medical grounds and is sup ported by a medical certificate by a competent medi- cal authority ; fry) Extraordinary leave without pay and allowances up-tO a m a x i m u m period of one hundred and ci:.1 in one term of office. 6. Leave Sanctioning Authority.—The President of India shall be the_ authority competent to sanction leave to the Presiding On;cer of the Appellate Tribunal. 7. Pension/Provident 1-UIKI.—In case a, serving Judge or an Officer of :he Central Government or State Government is holding the post of Presiding O S c e r . the Service rendered in Appellate Tribunal will count for pension to be drawn in accordance with the rules of tbc service to which he behsnps He shall also be governed by the provisions of the General Provident, Fund (Central Services J Rules, I960, i n all other cases, a person shall be entitled to Contributory Provider1 Fund (India) Rules, 3 962. 8- Travelling Allowances.—The Presiding Officer of an Appellate Tribunal while on tour or on i r a n k e r (including Ibe journey undertaken t o join the Appellate Tribunal or on Ice expiry of his term with the Appellate Tribunal to proceed to his home town) shall be entitled t o the travelling allow- ances. daily allowances, transportation of p r r s o m ! effects en'.! otiicr similar matters at the same scales and at the same ra'es as are prescribed in the High Court Judnc (Travelling Allow- ances) Rules. 1956. 9. Leave Travel Concession.—The Presidinp Officii of ai Appellate Tribunal shall be entitled to leave travel c o n c e s s i t at the same rates and at the same scale as are applicable lr a lodge of a Hi.r.h Court in tin Stale in which the App-ellate Tribunal is located. 10. Facility of conveyance.—The Presiding Officer cf an Appellate Tribunal shall be entitled t o a staff car and one hundred and fifty litres of pciro! e*ery month or actual con sumption of petrol per month, whichever is le*s 11. Accommodation.— (1) Every person appointed as a Presiding Officer of an Appellate Tribunal shall be clicrii-Ie. subject to availability. to the use or opciiil resider I on the general pool accommoda'ion of the t \ p e admissible to a G r o u p - A ' officer of the Central Government, who i- v.n•:•- ing at t h e place where such Appellate Tribunal is located end drav-.inc an equivalent pay on payment of.the licence fee at ihe rates specified by the C t n t r n l ' G o v e r n m e n t from tim"- to time. : EXTRAORDINARY [PART I I — S E C . 3(i)] , (21 Where the Piesiding Officer of an Appellate T r i b u n e occupies an official residence beyond permissible period, he stall be liable to pa> additional licence lee or penal rent, as the case may be, anil he shall be liable to eviction in Itccujdejutc v\i;ii lite rules applicable lo Central Government Servants. {}) Where the Presiding Officer of an Appellate Tribunal d o c i j avail o.r facility of official residence under sun- rule [1.1, lie shall be entitled lo House Rem Allowance as admissible to Group ' A ' officers of the Genital Government drawing equivalent pay. 12. Facilities for medical treatment.—The Presiding Offi- cer ol an Appellate Tribunal shall be entitled lo medical itcatmenl and hospital facilities as provided in the Contri- butory Health Services Scheme P.ules, 1954 and in places where IDC Central Health Services Scheme is not in operation, the said Presiding OJficer shall be entitled to the facilities ns provid-d in the Central Services.Medical Aitendance Rules, 1944. 13. Residuary Provision.—Mailers relating to ihe condl lions of services of the Presiding; Officer of an Appellate Tri- bunal viMi respect to whicii to express provision has been made in these rules shall be re/erred in each case to the Central Government for its decision and the decision of the Central Government thereon shall be bindine on the said Presiding Officer. 14.
